Using location for contextual suggestions Ask Claude for location-based recommendations (for example: "What restaurants are near me?"). Ask Claude for location-based recommendations (for example: "What restaurants are near me?"). Claude will request access to your location if not already granted. Claude will request access to your location if not already granted. Based on your location, Claude provides relevant suggestions. Based on your location, Claude provides relevant suggestions. You can view locations on a map and get navigation directions. You can view locations on a map and get navigation directions. Example: "Find nearby parks for jogging." Displaying locations with Maps When Claude suggests locations, they can be displayed on an interactive map. When Claude suggests locations, they can be displayed on an interactive map. Tap on any location to see more details. Tap on any location to see more details. Select a location to open it in Google Maps for navigation. Select a location to open it in Google Maps for navigation. Get turn-by-turn directions directly from the map. Get turn-by-turn directions directly from the map. Example: "Show me hardware stores within five miles on a map." Managing your calendar Ask Claude to check your schedule or create events. Ask Claude to check your schedule or create events. Claude will request calendar access if not already granted. Claude will request calendar access if not already granted. For checking availability: Claude reads your calendar and identifies free slots. For checking availability: Claude reads your calendar and identifies free slots. For creating events: Claude prepares event details and adds them to your calendar. For creating events: Claude prepares event details and adds them to your calendar. You can edit or delete events as needed. You can edit or delete events as needed. Examples: "What meetings do I have tomorrow?" "What meetings do I have tomorrow?" "Schedule a team lunch for next Thursday at noon." "Schedule a team lunch for next Thursday at noon." "Create a recurring workout session every Tuesday and Thursday at 6 AM."
